summary_one_line: Input must steer UpdateIp6Route to return an error code that is neither kErrorNone nor kErrorAddressQuery, requiring specific magic bytes in IPv6 header fields.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An IPv6 message payload whose header fields drive UpdateIp6Route() to return an error code that is neither kErrorNone nor kErrorAddressQuery (e.g. kErrorDrop / kErrorNoRoute / kErrorInvalidArgs). Concretely, the W seeds carry a fixed multi-byte literal at offsets 0x19-0x1f equal to `fd de ad 00 be ef 00` (a fd00::dead:beef-style IPv6 ULA prefix block) plus an early-offset header signature (`66 65 5c` = `fe\` at 0x01-0x03) — these bytes are constant across all 10 W seeds.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: cmplog/vpc winners contain the exact 7-byte run `fd de ad 00 be ef 00` at offsets 0x19-0x1f in 10/10 seeds, and the `66 65` (`fe`) signature at offsets 0x01-0x02 in 10/10 seeds. With these constants in place, switch(error) at line 571 falls through case kErrorNone and case kErrorAddressQuery and reaches `default:` at line 582.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive/vp blocking seeds never produce the `fd de ad 00 be ef 00` run (loser bytes at 0x19-0x1f are dominated by `ff ff ff 00 00 00 00` or other random patterns) and lack the `fe\` header signature. Without these literal byte runs, UpdateIp6Route returns kErrorNone (the dominant case at line 573 in the loser cov dump: T=105 F=0) and ExitNow() fires before reaching the default arm.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: I2SRandReplace in cmplog/vpc reads operands from the CmpLogObserver buffer populated by trace_cmp* callbacks inside UpdateIp6Route() and Ip6 header parsing; the integer/memory comparisons against the ULA prefix constants (0xfd 0xde 0xad 0xbe 0xef) and the header magic are captured and substituted into the input via I2SRandReplace. naive/vp lack I2SRandReplace and therefore cannot synthesize these multi-byte literal constants within budget — chaining 4-7 byte runs together at fixed offsets requires direct operand substitution, which only cmplog/vpc provide through I2SRandReplace.

evidence_trail.claim: Both decisive pairs at this branch are under the I2S technique delta.
The second decisive pair is also under I2S delta with cmplog winning over naive.
The blocker is the default arm of the switch(error) statement on UpdateIp6Route's return value.

Loser inputs overwhelmingly take the kErrorNone arm (T=105 F=0) while winner inputs take the default arm (T=19 F=60 on the kErrorNone case means most fall through to default; L=T=0 F=105 means loser never reaches default).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 573  T=60 F=19  T=105 F=0  case kErrorNone:

evidence_trail.claim: Loser never reaches the default arm at line 582 (W=T=19, L=T=0).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 582  T=19 F=60  T=0 F=105  default:  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: All 10 winner seeds contain the literal byte fd at offset 0x19, while only 1 of 10 loser seeds does.
All 10 winner seeds carry the literal byte de at offset 0x1a, while loser seeds dominate with 53 (`S`).
All 10 winner seeds carry the literal byte ad at offset 0x1b, while losers carry ff or 00.
All 10 winner seeds carry be ef 00 at offsets 0x1d-0x1f, never present together in losers.
All 10 winner seeds share the literal `f` (0x66) byte at offset 0x01 and `e` (0x65) at offset 0x02 (`fe` signature) — losers do not.
cmplog and value_profile_cmplog instrumentation captures CMP operands per execution and I2SRandReplace mutator substitutes those operands into the input.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile uses Hamming-distance CMP_MAP gradient but lacks I2SRandReplace, so it cannot directly substitute multi-byte literals.
cmplog winning trial-1 has 10/10 resolution while naive has 9/10 blocking and value_profile 10/10 blocking, attributable to I2S delta.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 8526 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 2d0d9b87803c3479` and observed an I2S-floor row (mutation_op = -) at depth 6 (ancestor 4578ba613b6d1a35). Cross-checked seed 23ef6000356aac6c — same dash-row ancestor 4578ba613b6d1a35 appears at depth 8. Two independent winning cmplog seeds converge on the same I2S-floor ancestor, confirming I2SRandReplace contributed to the lineage.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: If a synthetic harness replaces UpdateIp6Route()'s error-returning code path with one whose distinguishing inputs are NOT compared as multi-byte literals (e.g. error returned when an accumulated checksum equals a runtime value), then cmplog should NOT retain its >=8/10 win over naive — if it still wins, the mechanism is not I2S substitution of constants but something else.

weakest_evidence_point: I am inferring from BYTE DIFF that the fd-de-ad-be-ef and `fe` patterns are operands of integer/memory CMPs inside UpdateIp6Route — I have not directly traced which CMP instruction the I2S find substituted into. The patterns are consistent with IPv6 ULA prefix and link-local SLAAC tokens but the exact CMP site is unconfirmed.
